meta_note: All methods are exposed as gRPC procedures on the AuthService package. Callers are the API Gateway only. Auth context is passed via gRPC Metadata, not request fields.

conventions.metadata_access_token: x-token: <accessToken>  — decoded into metadata.user by TokenGuard when @TokenMeta('access_token') is applied
conventions.metadata_otp_token: x-token: <otpToken>  — short-lived OTP-scoped JWT; decoded into metadata.user by TokenGuard when @TokenMeta('otp_token') is applied
conventions.metadata_refresh_token: x-token: <refreshToken>  — decoded into metadata.user (session payload) by TokenGuard when @TokenMeta('refresh_token') is applied
conventions.no_metadata: Method accepts no metadata guard; identity is embedded in the request message itself (e.g. twoFactorToken field) or the operation is public

rpc_errors.UNAUTHENTICATED: Invalid, expired, or missing token / wrong credentials
rpc_errors.ALREADY_EXISTS: Resource conflict (duplicate email, token still valid, identical password, etc.)
rpc_errors.FAILED_PRECONDITION: Pre-condition not met (e.g. email already verified)

register.description: Register a new user with email and password. Hashes the password, persists the user, and dispatches an OTP to the provided email. Returns the new user record and a short-lived emailToken (otp_token) for the subsequent verifyEmail call.

input.password: string (required) — min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 symbol

output.emailToken: string — otp_token JWT; pass as x-token to verifyEmail

verifyEmail.description: Verify a user's email by checking the 6-digit OTP. Requires the emailToken from Register as x-token. Creates the first session and returns access + refresh tokens.
verifyEmail.authentication: x-token: <emailToken>  (otp_token) — metadata.user carries the token payload

input.otp: string (required) — 6-digit code from the verification email

resendVerifyEmailToken.description: Delete the previous pending email-verification token and issue a fresh one. Dispatches a new OTP to the user's email.

throws.ALREADY_EXISTS: User is already verified, or a valid token still exists and has not expired

login.description: Authenticate with email and password. Returns full auth tokens on success. If 2FA is enabled, returns requiresTwoFactor: true and a twoFactorToken instead — the caller must route the user to the verifyTwoFactor step.

_2fa_required.twoFactorToken: string — short-lived JWT (5 min); pass to verifyTwoFactor

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Invalid credentials or account locked after too many failed attempts

forgotPassword.description: Initiate a password reset. Dispatches a 6-digit OTP to the user's email if found. Always returns successfully to prevent user enumeration.

output.email: string — echoed back, or empty string if the user was not found

resendForgotPasswordToken.description: Resend the password reset OTP after the previous one has expired.

throws.ALREADY_EXISTS: A valid reset token already exists and has not yet expired

verifyPasswordToken.description: Verify the 6-digit OTP from the forgot-password email. Returns a short-lived passwordToken (otp_token) to pass as x-token when calling resetPassword.

output.passwordToken: string — otp_token JWT; pass as x-token to resetPassword

resetPassword.description: Set a new password using the passwordToken from verifyPasswordToken. Requires that token as x-token in metadata.
resetPassword.authentication: x-token: <passwordToken>  (otp_token) — metadata.user carries the token payload

input.newPassword: string (required) — min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 symbol

throws.ALREADY_EXISTS: New password is identical to the current password

validateToken.description: Validate an access token and return the associated user record. Used by the gateway to authenticate incoming requests before proxying to downstream services.
validateToken.authentication: x-token: <accessToken>  (access_token) — metadata.user carries the decoded payload

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Access token missing, invalid, or expired

refreshToken.description: Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair. The old refresh token is invalidated (rotation). Requires the refresh token as x-token in metadata.
refreshToken.authentication: x-token: <refreshToken>  (refresh_token) — metadata.user carries the session payload

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Refresh token invalid, expired, or session not found

loginWithGoogle.description: Sign in or register using a Google id_token from the client-side OAuth flow. Verifies the token against Google's public keys, then creates or finds the user and returns auth tokens.

input.idToken: string (required) — Google id_token from the client-side OAuth response

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Google id_token invalid, expired, or signature mismatch

initiateTwoFactorSetup.description: Generate a TOTP secret and otpauth URI for the authenticated user. 2FA is not yet active until confirmTwoFactorSetup succeeds. Render the URI as a QR code for the user to scan.

output.secret: string — base32 TOTP secret (show as manual-entry fallback)
output.otpauthUri: string — otpauth://totp/... URI for QR code rendering

confirmTwoFactorSetup.description: Confirm 2FA setup by verifying the first TOTP code from the authenticator app. Activates 2FA on the account and returns 10 one-time backup codes — displayed only once.

input.code: string (required) — exactly 6-digit TOTP code from the authenticator app

backupCodes: string — 10 × 8-character backup codes, shown once only

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Access token invalid, or TOTP code incorrect / expired

disableTwoFactor.description: Disable TOTP 2FA on the account. Requires the current TOTP code to prevent unauthorised disablement. Wipes the stored secret and all backup codes.

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Access token invalid, or TOTP code incorrect

verifyTwoFactor.description: Complete a 2FA login challenge with a TOTP code or backup code. Identity is proven via the twoFactorToken embedded in the request (no metadata guard). Backup codes are single-use.
verifyTwoFactor.authentication: None — identity proven via twoFactorToken field in the request

input.twoFactorToken: string (required) — short-lived JWT from login when 2FA is required
input.code: string (required) — 6-digit TOTP code or 8-character backup code
input.deviceId: string (optional) — client device identifier for session metadata
input.userAgent: string (optional) — HTTP User-Agent for session metadata
input.ipAddress: string (optional) — client IP address for session metadata
input.location: string (optional) — geographic location for session metadata

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: twoFactorToken invalid/expired, or TOTP/backup code incorrect

changePassword.description: Change the authenticated user's password. Verifies the current password first. Drops all active sessions on success and sends a security alert email.

input.newPassword: string (required) — min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 symbol

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Access token invalid, or current password incorrect
throws.ALREADY_EXISTS: New password is identical to the current password

initiateEmailChange.description: Begin an email change. Verifies password ownership, checks the new email is not already taken, and dispatches a 6-digit OTP to the new address. Complete the change via verifyEmailChange.

input.newEmail: string (required) — valid email address; stored lowercase

output.newEmail: string — the address the OTP was dispatched to

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Access token invalid, or current password incorrect
throws.ALREADY_EXISTS: New email is already registered to another account

verifyEmailChange.description: Complete an email change by submitting the OTP sent to the new address. Updates the email, deletes the pending token, drops all sessions, and sends a security alert to the old address.

input.otp: string (required) — 6-digit code sent to the new email address

throws.UNAUTHENTICATED: Access token invalid, or OTP incorrect/expired
throws.ALREADY_EXISTS: Target email was claimed by another account between initiation and verification
